title: The Video Editing Canvas
kills: The editing grind. Exporting to a separate NLE, scrubbing a timeline for an hour, and dreading the cut after you shoot.
headline: Edit the whole video. Without leaving the canvas.
subcopy: Drop your clips on the node canvas and shape the edit visually in one place. No round-trip to a separate editor, no project-file juggling. The edit lives beside the same pipeline that wrote the script and made the b-roll.
imageAlt: Papercraft editing timeline with stacked clip blocks and a paper razor.
imageSubject: A folded-paper editing timeline: a horizontal paper track with stacked paper clip-blocks of varying lengths, a tall paper playhead bar, and a small folded paper razor mid-cut over one clip, lit warm gold.

title: The Script Factory
kills: The blank page. The 3-hour what-do-I-even-post spiral.
headline: Production-ready scripts. Not blank pages.
subcopy: Pick a character, mix the ingredients - pain points, hooks, monetization, script type - and Breadstick writes a script good enough to paste straight into ElevenLabs. Persuasion psychology baked in: Life-Force 8, emotional-journey beats, the reframes that convert.
imageAlt: Papercraft typewriter unspooling a paper script.
imageSubject: A folded-paper vintage typewriter with a long paper script-scroll curling out of it, a few crisp folded pages fanned beside it.

title: One Canvas, Whole Pipeline
kills: 14 browser tabs and a spreadsheet holding your pipeline together.
headline: Your whole pipeline. One canvas.
subcopy: Drag-and-wire nodes - characters, generators, video, carousels, editing, posting - connected like a circuit board. Trace a script from idea to posted reel without leaving the screen.
imageAlt: Papercraft node graph wired with paper ribbons.
imageSubject: A papercraft circuit board / node graph with small folded paper cards linked by woven paper ribbon-wires, the active nodes lit warm gold.

title: Characters That Stay Consistent
kills: The face that morphs every video and the voice that never matches.
headline: Characters with a face, a voice, and a memory.
subcopy: Built-in operators across proven niches - and unlimited custom characters from one form. Consistent faces from Sora 2 cameos, consistent ElevenLabs voices, locked continuity across every clip. Your creator stops looking like five different people.
imageAlt: Three papercraft character masks on stands.
imageSubject: A row of three distinct origami character paper-masks on little stands, each clearly a different person, same paper family.

title: UGC Video Lane
kills: The obviously AI plastic look that gets zero saves.
headline: Talking-head UGC that reads as real.
subcopy: Character to script to lip-synced clips with Kling 3.0 and the iPhone-real V4 prompt rules: micro-shake, skin texture, hand safety, mid-thought delivery. The anti-uncanny pipeline, automated.
imageAlt: Papercraft phone on a tripod filming a paper person.
imageSubject: A folded-paper smartphone on a tiny paper tripod filming a small paper person mid-gesture, a paper recording dot in the corner.

title: 16-Gami Explainer Art
kills: Boring stock b-roll and flat slideshow art.
headline: The papercraft look that makes people stop.
subcopy: Breadstick's visual DNA - realistic 16-bit origami stills, character-locked across every shot, animated with Seedance 2.0. The exact style of the tiles you are looking at right now.
imageAlt: Paper hands folding a glowing origami crane.
imageSubject: A pair of paper hands caught mid-fold, lifting a glowing origami crane out of a flat sheet: the brand's signature folding-into-life moment.

title: Carousel Engine
kills: Hand-stitching 8 slides in Canva at midnight.
headline: Branded carousels in one render.
subcopy: Topic in, multi-slide carousel out - branded templates and a compositor that drops live video into the slide art zones. Save-bait that looks designed, not dumped.
imageAlt: Fanned stack of papercraft carousel slides.
imageSubject: A fanned stack of paper slide-cards spread like a hand of cards, each a slightly different folded layout.

title: Captions and Overlays
kills: The caption pass. Burning subtitles by hand, lining up lower-thirds, and faking designed text.
headline: Captions and motion graphics that pop.
subcopy: Dozens of overlay compositions - hook captions, lower-thirds, highlight sweeps, burst lines, beat-synced text. Synced to your audio, burned in clean. The polish that earns the rewatch, without the timeline tedium.
imageAlt: Papercraft lower-third banner and a burst of paper accent lines over a frame.
imageSubject: A paper lower-third banner and a folded paper burst of exclamation lines popping over a blank paper video frame.

title: Footage Lanes
kills: The dead hours of manual cutting, grading, and clipping.
headline: Drop raw footage. Get a finished cut.
subcopy: Shortform and longform processors take a raw POV or desk recording through transcribe, silence-cut, overlay, color grade, ready clips, and metadata. A render cache means you never pay twice for the same work.
imageAlt: Papercraft conveyor belt of film reels and a finished clip.
imageSubject: A paper conveyor belt carrying folded film-reel and clapperboard pieces, a finished glossy paper clip dropping off the end into a tray.

title: The Scoreboard
kills: Posting into the void with no idea what worked.
headline: It tells you what actually worked.
subcopy: Every post is tagged at birth by lane and angle. Breadstick pulls real performance, keeps a ledger, and rotates your A/B angles on a deterministic rule - leader gets the budget, losers get cut. Arithmetic, never a vibe. Your content compounds.
imageAlt: Papercraft bar chart with a gold leading bar.
imageSubject: A papercraft bar chart built from folded paper bars of rising height, the tallest bar gold and crowned with a tiny paper flag.

title: Distribution
kills: Copy-pasting the same post into three apps at the right time.
headline: One post. Every platform. Tracked.
subcopy: Schedule across TikTok, Instagram, and Facebook through Postiz and Blotato - and because every post is tagged at birth, the Scoreboard knows exactly which lane and angle earned the view.
imageAlt: Papercraft hub fanning arrows to three platform badges.
imageSubject: A central paper hub with folded paper arrows fanning out to three small paper platform badges: a music-note, a camera, and an f, all kraft-and-gold.

title: Run It From Your Phone
kills: Being chained to the desk to make anything.
headline: Your studio, in WhatsApp.
subcopy: Drive the pipeline by text or voice from anywhere. A real voice answers; an ambient buddy narrates jobs as they finish; tickets ping you when a render is done. The studio comes with you.
imageAlt: Papercraft phone with a chat bubble and a small paper astronaut.
imageSubject: A folded-paper phone showing a paper chat-bubble, with a tiny paper astronaut buddy peeking over the top edge.
